title: reseda spathulata
intro: Reseda spathulata, also known as Spotted Reseda, is a plant species belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae) and is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Reseda spathulata include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, as well as its potential to treat conditions such as eczema and dermatitis. Herbal preparations of Reseda spathulata can be made into infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically or taken internally under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
